canonical_truth.summary: A sustained heat wave makes foraging costly — the canopy fails to block, bark is warm before noon, and movement carries a physical price
canonical_truth.concrete_outcome: West spot yield reduced; both branches pay a heat penalty in food and health; alt1-on-time uses west terrain knowledge to route around the worst of it

perception_prompt.noticeable_surface: the canopy isn't blocking anything today — the light comes through wrong, the bark is warm to the touch before noon, and the usual foraging line feels twice as long

authorial_note.real_world_inspiration: hottest March on record for the Lower 48 states, widest margin ever recorded for any month — federal data released April 19 2026
authorial_note.translation_logic: extreme heat reshapes the cost-benefit of every movement; which branch has resources and range intelligence matters more when stillness and motion both carry a price
authorial_note.restraint_reason: heat enters through bark temperature and canopy failure, not through announcement — YY's body is the instrument
